name: Timcaek (Steamed Chocolate Pudding)
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon baking powder + 1 teaspoon baking soda
recipeInstructions:
1. Two days before cooking: Place the cherries in a bowl and cover with liquor of your choice. Let stand out of direct sunlight until ready to use.
2. Day Of:
a. Set up a large pot with a waterproof rack or mason jar ring in the bottom to place the pudding mold.
b. Fill the pot with water and bring it to a boil.
c. Butter the sides of a 1 1/2 quart pudding mold liberally and set aside.
d. In a medium bowl, dissolve almond paste in milk. Combine the milk mixture with beaten egg, molasses, and vanilla extract.
e. In a large bowl, combine flour, cocoa, baking powder, brown sugar, and salt. Mix well to break up any lumps. Add melted butter and mix.
f. Pour in the milk mixture and beat until combined. Fold in chocolate chips and dried cherries.
g. Place a round of buttered parchment over the mold and snap on the lid.
h. Carefully place the pudding onto the rack, ensuring the water level is about 1/3 of the way up the sides of the mold.
i. Place the pot back on heat until it reaches a gentle simmer, then reduce to low heat.
j. Allow the pot to simmer covered over low heat for one hour.
k. After an hour, carefully remove the pudding from the pot and place it on a wire rack to cool. Remove the lid and parchment when ready.
l. Invert the cake onto a serving plate while still warm. It should release easily from the mold.
m. Serve immediately with ice cream or lightly sweetened whipped cream. Alternatively, serve with hard sauce made using the same liquor as the cherries.
